description: Full export of agent_tasks. The local-db-expert is responsible for importing this into the octo_agents database using ON CONFLICT (agent_name, title) DO UPDATE to keep data in sync.

tasks.title: TASK 1 - Scaffold Phoenix project
tasks.description: Create a new Phoenix LiveView project with PostgreSQL adapter and Tailwind CSS configured.

tasks.title: TASK 2 - Agent schema and migration
tasks.description: Define Agent schema and Ecto migration with fields: id, name, folder_path, description, inserted_at.

tasks.title: TASK 3 - Task schema and migration
tasks.description: Define Task schema and migration with fields: id, agent_id, title, status, position, inserted_at.

tasks.title: TASK 4 - Agent loader
tasks.description: Build module that scans backlog/, parses each agent_init.md and TODO.txt, and upserts records into the DB.

tasks.title: TASK 5 - AgentList LiveView
tasks.description: Build LiveView listing all agents with streams, showing name, task count, and a status badge per agent.

tasks.title: TASK 6 - AgentDetail LiveView
tasks.description: Build LiveView showing agent info and task list with checkboxes to mark tasks as in_progress or done.

tasks.title: TASK 7 - InteractionPanel LiveComponent
tasks.description: Build live component with a prompt text input, run button, and scrollable output display area.

tasks.title: UI-008 - AgentRunner module
tasks.description:
Scope: Create lib/octo_dashboard/agent_runner.ex and lib/octo_dashboard/runner_supervisor.ex. Add RunnerSupervisor to application.ex children if missing.

Done when:
- AgentRunner.start_run(%Agent{}, prompt) starts a temporary GenServer under RunnerSupervisor
- Port opens with {:spawn_executable, claude_path} and options [:binary, :exit_status, {:line, 4096}]
- Each {:eol, line} is broadcast to "agent:<id>:runner" via OctoDashboard.PubSub
- {:exit_status, code} stops the GenServer with :normal
- claude_path read from Application.fetch_env!(:octo_dashboard, :claude_path) — never hardcoded
- RunnerSupervisor is a DynamicSupervisor with restart: :temporary per child

Commit: feat: add AgentRunner GenServer with port-based claude CLI streaming

tasks.title: UI-009 - Wire DashboardLive to AgentRunner
tasks.description:
Scope: Modify lib/octo_dashboard_web/live/dashboard_live.ex only.

Done when:
- handle_info({:interaction_prompt_submitted, prompt}) calls AgentRunner.start_run/2 for selected agent
- Selecting an agent subscribes socket to "agent:<id>:runner" via Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe
- handle_info({:runner_chunk, interaction_id, line}) appends line to output_lines, capped at 300
- handle_info({:runner_finished, interaction_id, exit_code, _}) sets runner_state to "idle" and puts flash
- Switching agents unsubscribes previous topic before subscribing new one
- runner_state assign is "idle" | "running" and controls submit button disabled state

Commit: feat: wire DashboardLive to AgentRunner via PubSub for real-time output streaming

tasks.title: UI-010 - Persist interaction history
tasks.description:
Scope: Create lib/octo_dashboard/dashboard/interaction.ex and one migration file. Add create_interaction/1, complete_interaction/2, list_interactions/2, get_interaction/1 to dashboard.ex.

Done when:
- Migration creates interactions table with: id, agent_id (FK, on_delete: :delete_all), prompt (text not null), full_response (text default ""), exit_code (integer), started_at (utc_datetime_usec not null), completed_at (utc_datetime_usec), inserted_at
- Index on agent_id and inserted_at
- Dashboard.create_interaction/1 inserts on run start
- Dashboard.complete_interaction/2 updates full_response, exit_code, completed_at on exit
- InteractionPanel displays last 10 interactions for selected agent
- mix ecto.migrate applies cleanly

Commit: feat: add Interaction schema and persist agent run history to PostgreSQL

tasks.title: UI-011 - Tailwind layout
tasks.description:
Scope: Modify dashboard_live.ex and interaction_panel_component.ex only.

Done when:
- Three-column layout on xl+: agent sidebar (~340px), detail (flex-1), interaction panel (~540px)
- Single column on mobile
- Agent sidebar: bg-white/85 backdrop-blur rounded-[1.75rem] border border-white/70 shadow-xl
- Interaction panel: bg-slate-950 text-slate-100 rounded-[1.75rem] shadow-2xl
- Stats row: Agents (sky), Queued Tasks (amber), Active Tasks (emerald)
- Task badges: todo=slate, in_progress=amber, done=emerald
- Agent badges: idle=slate, queued=sky, active=amber, done=emerald
- Sync Backlog button in header
- Empty state when no agent selected

Commit: style: apply three-column operator layout with glass cards and dark interaction panel

tasks.title: UI-012 - Boot test
tasks.description:
Scope: No code changes unless broken. Fix only the single file that is wrong.

Done when:
- docker compose up --build starts app and db with no blocking errors
- Migrations apply on boot
- localhost:4000 loads without crash
- Sync Backlog loads agents from filesystem
- Selecting agent shows its tasks
- No compile warnings about missing modules

Notes: Fixed boot issues in dev.sh, core_components.ex, dashboard_live.ex, agent_runner.ex, dev.exs, docker-compose.yml. watchman missing is non-fatal.

Commit: fix: <describe what was broken> (only if code changes needed)

tasks.title: UI-013 - Validate claude CLI invocation
tasks.description:
Scope: Modify lib/octo_dashboard/agent_runner.ex only (args list and Port options).

Done when:
- claude --help run on dev machine to confirm available flags and argument format
- Prompt passing method confirmed (positional arg, flag value, or stdin)
- Current impl ["--print", prompt] verified or corrected
- Real test run via dashboard UI produces visible streamed output in interaction panel
- Output arrives line by line, not buffered until exit

Status: claude --help confirmed -p/--print flag. Remaining gap: end-to-end test inside Docker requires claude CLI binary mounted or installed in the container.

Commit: fix: correct claude CLI args for port-based invocation (only if args were wrong)

tasks.title: UI-017 - Display agent tasks from octo_agents DB
tasks.description:
Scope: Add a read-only query against octo_agents and display results in the agent detail panel.
Modify dashboard_live.ex and dashboard.ex only. No writes to octo_agents from the dashboard.

Done when:
- Selecting an agent queries octo_agents WHERE agent_name matches the agent folder slug
- Results shown in agent detail panel with clear visual grouping by status: todo, in_progress, done
- Each task row shows: title, status badge, priority
- If octo_agents is unreachable or returns no rows, section shows "No DB tasks" without crashing
- Data is read-only — no editing, claiming, or status changes from the dashboard
- Data loaded once on agent selection, no polling

Commit: feat: display agent tasks from octo_agents DB in agent detail panel

tasks.title: UI-014 - Fix stream_count helper
tasks.description:
Scope: Modify lib/octo_dashboard_web/live/dashboard_live.ex only.

Done when:
- stream_count(@streams.tasks) replaced with @task_count (integer assign)
- task_count set in load_dashboard as length(selected_agent.tasks), 0 when no agent selected
- Private stream_count/1 function removed if no longer used
- agent_count already uses this pattern; identical pattern applied to task_count
- Displayed task count updates correctly when switching agents

Commit: fix: replace stream_count/1 with tracked task_count assign

tasks.title: UI-015 - DONE.txt support in Loader
tasks.description:
Scope: Modify lib/octo_dashboard/dashboard/loader.ex only, specifically parse_tasks/1.

Done when:
- Loader reads Tasks/DONE.txt if it exists, using same [x]/[ ] pattern
- DONE.txt tasks appended after TODO.txt tasks with continuing positions
- k3s-setup-expert and usb-drive-setup-expert show completed tasks after sync
- Missing DONE.txt handled gracefully (no crash)
- Position-based upsert prevents duplicate rows

Commit: feat: include DONE.txt tasks in agent task loader

tasks.title: UI-016 - Task status sync safety
tasks.description:
Scope: Modify lib/octo_dashboard/dashboard/loader.ex only, specifically sync_tasks/2.

Done when:
- Existing DB tasks with status in_progress or done are NOT reset to todo on sync
- File marker [x] with DB status todo updates DB to done
- File marker [ ] with DB status in_progress or done preserves DB status
- New tasks get status from file marker
- Logic in private merged_status(existing_task, parsed_status)

Commit: fix: preserve in_progress and done task statuses across backlog syncs

tasks.title: DB-001 - Verify PostgreSQL is accessible
tasks.description:
Scope: No code changes. Verification only.

Done when:
- docker compose up db (from Dashboard/) starts cleanly with no errors
- docker exec octo_dashboard_db pg_isready -U postgres returns "accepting connections"
- psql postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/octo_dashboard_dev -c "SELECT 1" succeeds from host machine
- Host port 5432 is not blocked
- PostgreSQL version and database existence confirmed

Commit: none (verification only)

tasks.title: DB-002 - Run dashboard migrations
tasks.description:
Scope: No migration files to create. Run existing migrations inside app container.

Done when:
- All three migrations applied: create_agents, create_tasks, create_interactions
- \dt on octo_dashboard_dev shows: schema_migrations, agents, tasks, interactions
- \d agents, \d tasks, \d interactions show correct columns and constraints
- All three migration timestamps recorded in schema_migrations
- If migration fails: report exact error, do not fix files without user approval

Commit: none (no files changed)

tasks.title: DB-003 - Design agent task-pull schema
tasks.description:
Scope: Write DB/SCHEMA_DESIGN.md only. No SQL yet.

Done when:
- SCHEMA_DESIGN.md documents the octo_agents database schema
- Table: agent_tasks (id, agent_name, title, description, status, priority, claimed_at, completed_at, created_at)
- Status values and transitions defined: todo -> in_progress -> done
- Indexes specified alongside query patterns they support
- Atomic claim strategy addressed (SKIP LOCKED)
- Unique constraint on (agent_name, title) for idempotent seeding

Commit: docs: add agent task-pull schema design

tasks.title: DB-004 - Create bootstrap SQL
tasks.description:
Scope: Write DB/bootstrap_agent_db.sql only. Must be idempotent (safe to run multiple times).

Done when:
- File creates octo_agents database if not exists
- All tables with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS including UNIQUE (agent_name, title)
- All indexes with CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS
- Idempotent unique constraint migration via DO $$ block
- Runs with: psql postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/postgres -f bootstrap_agent_db.sql
- Running twice produces no errors
- No DROP statements

Commit: feat: add bootstrap SQL for octo_agents database and agent task schema

tasks.title: DB-005 - Apply agent task-pull schema
tasks.description:
Scope: No file changes. Run bootstrap_agent_db.sql and verify result.

Done when:
- bootstrap_agent_db.sql runs with no errors
- \dt on octo_agents shows agent_tasks table
- \d agent_tasks shows correct column types, NOT NULL constraints, unique constraint
- \di shows expected indexes present
- If file had errors: fix bootstrap_agent_db.sql and include fix in commit

Commit: none if no changes needed; fix: correct bootstrap SQL errors if file needed fixing

tasks.title: DB-006 - Document agent query interface
tasks.description:
Scope: Write DB/AGENT_QUERIES.md only.

Done when:
- Documents DATABASE_URL: postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/octo_agents
- Exact SQL with placeholders for: claim next task (SKIP LOCKED), mark done, list my tasks, add a task
- Explains agent_name convention (folder name slug, e.g. "dashboard-ui-expert")
- Short enough to read in under two minutes

Commit: docs: add AGENT_QUERIES.md with agent task SQL interface

tasks.title: DB-007 - Seed agent task data
tasks.description:
Scope: Write DB/seed_agent_tasks.sql only. Run and verify.

Done when:
- seed_agent_tasks.sql contains all real tasks from each agent backlog
- Uses ON CONFLICT (agent_name, title) DO NOTHING for idempotency
- Correct statuses: done for completed tasks, todo for pending
- Claim next task query returns correct highest-priority todo row per agent
- SELECT count(*) FROM agent_tasks WHERE status = 'todo' matches expected count

Commit: feat: add seed SQL with real agent task data

tasks.title: TASK 1 - Enable cgroup memory
tasks.description: Update active boot cmdline on Raspberry Pi to enable memory cgroup support required by K3s.

tasks.title: TASK 2 - Reboot Pi
tasks.description: Reboot after kernel command line change so memory cgroup settings take effect.

tasks.title: TASK 3 - Install K3s
tasks.description: Install K3s using the official install script for a single-node server with --disable traefik.

tasks.title: TASK 4 - Enable and start K3s service
tasks.description: Enable and start the k3s systemd service, verify it is active via systemctl status.

tasks.title: TASK 5 - Verify K3s is running
tasks.description: Check service state and confirm k3s version v1.34.6+k3s1.

tasks.title: TASK 6 - Configure kubeconfig for SSH user
tasks.description: Copy admin kubeconfig to SSH user home directory, set KUBECONFIG=$HOME/.kube/config.

tasks.title: TASK 7 - Verify cluster health
tasks.description: Confirm node mob is Ready and core system pods are Running.

tasks.title: STEP 1 - Discover attached block devices
tasks.description: Run lsblk, identify USB vs SD card, confirm device with user if multiple USB drives detected.

tasks.title: STEP 2 - Inspect the target device
tasks.description: Run blkid and fdisk -l, report filesystem type, label, UUID, partition status.

tasks.title: STEP 3 - Confirm intent with the user
tasks.description: Show device path, current contents, planned action, data loss warning. Wait for explicit yes before proceeding.

tasks.title: STEP 4 - Partition and/or format
tasks.description: Only if needed: create GPT partition table and ext4 filesystem. Skip if already compatible.

tasks.title: STEP 5 - Create the mount point
tasks.description: mkdir -p /mnt/usb-data.

tasks.title: STEP 6 - Mount the device temporarily
tasks.description: Mount device to /mnt/usb-data, verify with df -h and findmnt.

tasks.title: STEP 7 - Add to /etc/fstab using UUID
tasks.description: Retrieve UUID via blkid, add fstab entry with nofail, validate with mount -a.

tasks.title: STEP 8 - Create service-specific directory structure
tasks.description: mkdir -p /mnt/usb-data/postgresql/data.

tasks.title: STEP 9 - Validate and apply ownership/permissions
tasks.description: postgres user not found at setup time. Directories left with root ownership. Must re-run chown/chmod after PostgreSQL is installed.

tasks.title: STEP 10 - Final verification
tasks.description: Run ls -la, stat, df -h. Report results and confirm USB is ready.
